subject: Chemistry
solution:
The conductivity (specific conductance), $\kappa$, is given by the product of conductance and cell constant:

$\kappa = G \times G^*$

$\kappa = 1.9 \times 10^{-3} \text{ S} \times 1.3 \text{ cm}^{-1} = 2.47 \times 10^{-3} \text{ S cm}^{-1}$

The molar conductivity, $\Lambda_m$, is related to the conductivity and molarity $C$ by the formula:

$\Lambda_m = \dfrac{\kappa \times 1000}{C}$

Substituting the given values:

$123.5 = \dfrac{2.47 \times 10^{-3} \times 1000}{C}$

$123.5 = \dfrac{2.47}{C}$

$C = \dfrac{2.47}{123.5} = 0.02 \text{ mol L}^{-1}$

Molarity $C$ can also be expressed in terms of mass percentage $x$ (w/w), density $d$, and molar mass $M$:

$C = \dfrac{x \times d \times 10}{M}$

Substituting the known values ($d = 1.0 \text{ g mL}^{-1}$, $M = 75 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$):

$0.02 = \dfrac{x \times 1.0 \times 10}{75}$

$1.5 = 10x$

$x = 0.15$

Expressing $x$ in the required format:

$x = 15 \times 10^{-2}$

The value is $15$.

Answer: $15$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The standard cell potential $E^\circ_{cell}$ is a thermodynamic property defined for a cell under standard conditions (concentrations of all species at $1\text{ M}$, pressure at $1\text{ bar}$, and a specific temperature).
For a Daniell cell, the reaction is $Zn(s) + Cu^{2+}(aq) \rightarrow Zn^{2+}(aq) + Cu(s)$.
The standard cell potential is given by $E^\circ_{cell} = E^\circ_{Cu^{2+}/Cu} - E^\circ_{Zn^{2+}/Zn}$.
Since $E^\circ_{cell}$ depends only on the nature of the electrodes, the nature of the electrolytes, and the temperature, it remains constant over time as long as the temperature is constant.
While the actual cell potential $E_{cell}$ changes as the reaction proceeds (due to changes in concentration according to the Nernst equation), the standard cell potential $E^\circ_{cell}$ is a constant value.
Therefore, the plot of $E^\circ_{cell}$ versus time is a horizontal line parallel to the time axis.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cell reaction which occurs:
12H2( g)+Fe3+ (aq.) ⟶H+(aq)+Fe2+ (aq.) 
Using Nernst' equation:

E=Eo-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒0.712=(0.771-0)-0.0591logFe2+Fe3+

⇒logFe2+Fe3+=(0.771-0712)0.059=1

⇒Fe2+Fe3+=10

subject: Chemistry
solution:
On applyinf Nernst equation-

E=E°-0.0591nlogQE=-1.23-0.05914log[H+]4
=1.23+0.0591×pH=-1.23+0.0591×5
=-1.23+0.2955=-0.9345V=-0.93V

The Nernst equation defines the relationship between cell potential to standard potential and to the activities of the electrically active (electroactive) species. It relates the effective concentrations (activities) of the components of a cell reaction to the standard cell potential.

subject: Chemistry
solution: The lead storage battery is as shown in diagram. Lead storage battery consists of lead anode and a grid of lead packed with lead oxide(PbO2) as cathode, a 38% solution of H2SO4 is used as an electrolyte. During charging of battery PbSO4 deposited on cathode is converted in PbO2 and PbSO4 deposited on anode is converted into Pb. The electrolyte used in battery is H2SO4 which is about 38% by mass.

Ecell∘=ECu2+Cu∘−EZn2+Zn∘=1.1 V

So If E=1.1 V no electron will flow

At E>1.1 V cell act as electrolytic cell and electron will flow from Cu to Zn.

At E<1.1 V cell act as electrochemical so Zn dissolve and Cu deposit.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Strongest reducing agent has the highest tendency to get oxidised. Higher the oxidation potential, higher is the tendency to get oxidised. Reduction potential of Cr3+ is least (ECr3+/Cro= -0.74V) which indicates that oxidation potential of Cr is highest (ECr/Cr3+o= 0.74V). Thus, Cr is strongest reducing agent.

solution:
For feasibility, check , Eocell=Ecathode(reduction)o-Eanode(oxidation)o>0

The Eo values when metal acts as anode and NO3- reaction is cathodic reaction.

For Vanadium metal, Ecello=0.97+1.19=2.16V

For Iron metal, Ecello=0.97+0.04=1.01V

For silver metal, Ecello=0.97-0.80=0.17V

For Gold metal, Ecello=0.97-1.140=-0.17V

For electrodes having oxidation potential greater than -0.97 V, Eocell>0

∴ Ag, Fe and V can be oxidised

subject: Chemistry
solution:
We have considered
$E^{\circ}_{[Sn(OH)_6]^{2-}/HSnO_2^-} = -0.9 \text{ V}$
$Pt | HSnO_2^-(aq), [Sn(OH)_6]^{2-}(aq), OH^-(aq) | Bi_2O_3(s) | Bi(s) |$
$0.5M \quad 0.05M$
$E^{\circ}_{cell} = +0.9 - 0.44 = 0.46 \text{ V}$
Oxidation Half :
$HSnO_2^- + H_2O + 3OH^- \rightarrow [Sn(OH)_6]^{2-} + 2e^-$
Reduction Half :
$Bi_2O_3 + 3H_2O + 6e^- \rightarrow 2Bi + 6OH^-$
---------------------------------------
$3HSnO_2^-(aq) + Bi_2O_3(s) + 6H_2O + 3OH^-(aq) \rightarrow 3[Sn(OH)_6]^{2-}(aq) + 2Bi(s)$
$E_{cell} = E^{\circ}_{cell} - \dfrac{0.059}{6} \log \dfrac{(0.5)^3}{(0.05)^3 \times [OH^-]^3}$
$0.2353 = 0.46 - \dfrac{0.059}{6} \times 3 \log \left[ \dfrac{10}{[OH^-]} \right]$
$\log \left[ \dfrac{10}{OH^-} \right] = \dfrac{2 \times 0.2247}{0.059} = 7.6$
$1 + pOH = 7.6$
$pOH = 6.6$
$pH = 14 - 6.6 = 7.4$
$pH = pK_{a_1} + \log \dfrac{[HCO_3^-]}{[H_2CO_3]}$
$7.4 = 6.11 + \log \dfrac{5x}{20}$
$1.29 = \log \dfrac{x}{4}$
$\dfrac{x}{4} = 19.5$
$x = 78$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
There is one mistake in Question paper.
Assuming concentration of solution is $0.2 \mathrm{M}$ instead of $0.02 \mathrm{M}$. Since resistance of $0.2 \mathrm{M}$ is $520 \Omega$.
$$
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{R} & =100 \Omega \\
\mathrm{K} & =\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}}\left(\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{a}}\right) \\
1.29 & =\frac{1}{100}\left(\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{a}}\right) \\
\left(\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{a}}\right) & =129 \mathrm{~m}^{-1} \\
\mathrm{R} & =520 \Omega, \mathrm{C}=0.2 \mathrm{M} \\
\mathrm{K} & =\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}}\left(\frac{\ell}{\mathrm{a}}\right)=\frac{1}{520}(129) \Omega^{-1} \mathrm{~m}^{-1} \\
\mu & =\mathrm{K} \times \mathrm{V}_{\text {in } \mathrm{cm}} \\
& =\frac{1}{520} \times 129 \times \frac{1000}{0.2} \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^3 \\
& =\frac{129}{520} \times \frac{1000}{0.2} \times 10^{-6} \\
& =1.24 \times 10^{-3} \\
& =12.4 \times 10^{-4}
\end{aligned}
$$

solution:
Given balanced equation is

60H⊝+C⊝l⟶ClO3-+3H2O+6e-

→10 gKClO3⇒10122.6 molKCO3 in obtained

→ from the above reaction, it is concluded that by
6 F charge 1 molKClO3 is obtained.

→ By the passage of 6 F charge =1 molKClO3

∴ By the passage of x×10×60×6096500F charge

=16×x×10×60×6096500

Now x×10×60×606×96500=10122.6

⇒x=10×96560×122.6=965735.6=1.311≃1

OR

W=EF×I×t

10=122.696500×6×x×10×3600

X=1.311
